title: The Smoking Jacket

jokes.text: You wished that Louis Lewis would die, so you don't have to give up your own kidney to Richard.
jokes.setup: Larry's birthday wish is being guessed at
jokes.punchline: The accusation that he'd wish for someone's death to avoid kidney donation
jokes.explanation: Strong dark comedy that perfectly captures Larry's selfish nature while introducing the kidney donor plot. The specificity of wishing for death to avoid medical responsibility is peak Larry David.

jokes.text: You think I would wish for somebody who's in a coma, who's practically a vegetable, to die, just so I wouldn't have to give up my own kidney?
jokes.setup: Larry is defending himself from the accusation
jokes.punchline: His overly specific denial that basically confirms the accusation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-incrimination through over-explanation. The 'practically a vegetable' detail makes it worse.

jokes.text: It's good to have a big friend, you can really smack him around. You know, look at that! Doesn't hurt him, doesn't feel anything.
jokes.setup: Someone got Larry a present
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately starts using his large friend as a punching bag
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy. The casualness of treating someone as a punching bag is appropriately Larry-esque but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: What? It's my present. Let go. Let go with the package.
jokes.setup: Someone is helping Larry unwrap his gift
jokes.punchline: Larry gets aggressive about unwrapping his own present
jokes.explanation: Petty Larry behavior over gift unwrapping. Shows his possessiveness but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: I have to confirm something before I give it to you.
jokes.setup: Someone has a birthday gift on hold
jokes.punchline: The mysterious confirmation needed creates anticipation
jokes.explanation: Sets up intrigue but not inherently funny on its own. Functional setup.

jokes.text: Well, at least it's not his hip. Because once it's in the hip, then you're gone. They all... they say you're gone.
jokes.setup: Discussion about someone's father's declining health
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact assessment about hip problems being fatal
jokes.explanation: Good Larry observation about aging. The certainty and folk wisdom delivery is authentic to character.

jokes.text: Come on. We'll be 'going to a movie' or something.
jokes.setup: Jeff inviting Larry to Playboy mansion
jokes.punchline: The obvious lie they'd tell their wives
jokes.explanation: Standard married guy deception humor. The air quotes are implied and relatable.

jokes.text: Did your parents ever mention anything to you about me being... adopted?
jokes.setup: Larry asking a serious question
jokes.punchline: The absurd adoption theory at his age
jokes.explanation: Great Larry neurosis. The pause before 'adopted' and the late-in-life identity crisis is perfectly him.

jokes.text: I hired a private investigator.
jokes.setup: Larry's adoption concerns
jokes.punchline: He's already taken dramatic action
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry escalation. Goes from suspicion to hiring a PI instantly. Economical reveal of how far he's gone.

jokes.text: So... how long do you think he has?
jokes.setup: Larry visiting someone in a coma
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking about death timeline
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Larry question. The bluntness about someone's death timeline is peak cringe.

jokes.text: If you had to pick one. Take a shot.
jokes.setup: Doctor won't estimate timeline for coma patient
jokes.punchline: Larry pressuring for a death prediction
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of inappropriate behavior. 'Take a shot' like it's a sports bet makes it worse.

jokes.text: What happens to the moustache in a coma?
jokes.setup: Discussion about coma patient care
jokes.punchline: Larry's fixation on facial hair maintenance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist question. The mundane concern about grooming while someone's dying is peak Larry observation.

jokes.text: Seems a pity though, that... these people like they... languish like this when the soul is crying to...
jokes.setup: Larry getting philosophical about the coma patient
jokes.punchline: His fake spiritual concern masking selfish motives
jokes.explanation: Larry's attempt at profundity to justify his real agenda. The awkward phrasing reveals his insincerity.

jokes.text: You pull that plug and I'll tell.
jokes.setup: Apparent confrontation about unplugging life support
jokes.punchline: Reveals someone was watching Larry's suspicious behavior
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setting up the blackmail plot. Creates tension about what Larry was actually doing.

jokes.text: No, it fell. It fell on the floor. I'm picking it up.
jokes.setup: Larry accused of unplugging life support
jokes.punchline: His obviously false excuse
jokes.explanation: Weak excuse that makes Larry look more guilty. Standard deflection humor but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I want to see a naked woman.
jokes.setup: Wilson's make-a-wish that was denied
jokes.punchline: The inappropriately direct sexual request
jokes.explanation: Blunt sexual request from sick kid creates uncomfortable comedy. The directness and Larry's predicament work well.

jokes.text: Hey, I almost did.
jokes.setup: Wilson saying imagine going through life without seeing a naked woman
jokes.punchline: Larry's admission about his own near-miss
jokes.explanation: Nice self-deprecating admission from Larry. Relatable and reveals character history efficiently.

jokes.text: Take me to your place.
jokes.setup: Larry saying his wife won't help with the naked woman request
jokes.punchline: Wilson's direct request to involve Larry's wife
jokes.explanation: Escalates the uncomfortable situation. Wilson's boldness creates more problems for Larry.

jokes.text: This is probably a birthday gift for me. I'll tell you why. 'Cause when we were younger, whenever it was my birthday my father would always send a gift to him so he wouldn't feel left out.
jokes.setup: Andy receives a smoking jacket from Larry's father
jokes.punchline: Larry's elaborate theory about why it's actually his gift
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry rationalization to get what he wants. The childhood gift theory shows his selfishness and creativity in justifying it.

jokes.text: I had a phone number. A very important number written on it.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about a magazine
jokes.punchline: Reveals he lost something important when it was thrown away
jokes.explanation: Sets up the magazine subplot. Creates stakes for later payoff but not inherently funny alone.

jokes.text: Because he took it in the bathroom and contaminated it.
jokes.setup: Why the magazine was thrown away
jokes.punchline: The gross but logical reason for disposal
jokes.explanation: Relatable domestic humor. The 'contaminated' terminology for bathroom reading is appropriately clinical and gross.

jokes.text: You fucking moron! Give me that jacket.
jokes.setup: Larry's argument about the smoking jacket
jokes.punchline: His explosive anger and name-calling over clothing
jokes.explanation: Larry's childish outburst over a jacket. The profanity and aggression over something trivial is peak Larry.

jokes.text: It's a big bowl of wrong.
jokes.setup: Larry describing the jacket situation
jokes.punchline: His peculiar phrase for injustice
jokes.explanation: Great Larry-ism. The food metaphor for moral outrage is original and quotable. Very much his voice.

jokes.text: You're already in the doghouse, you can't get in any more trouble, you might as well get your money's worth.
jokes.setup: Larry's relationship troubles
jokes.punchline: Jeff's logic about maximizing transgression value
jokes.explanation: Good male friendship logic. The economic approach to relationship problems is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: It's a 'double transgression' theory.
jokes.setup: Jeff's advice about getting in more trouble
jokes.punchline: His academic-sounding name for the concept
jokes.explanation: Excellent coined term that becomes important later. The pseudo-academic framing of bad behavior is clever and memorable.

jokes.text: You want to go to the Playboy mansion? Give me the jacket.
jokes.setup: Andy wanting to go to the mansion
jokes.punchline: Larry's transactional offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry deal-making. Uses someone's desires against them to get what he wants. Economical and character-driven.

jokes.text: I'm taking it to the drycleaner. Take it off.
jokes.setup: Larry got the jacket from Andy
jokes.punchline: Immediately demanding Andy remove it for cleaning
jokes.explanation: Larry's immediate control over the jacket. The pettiness of not even letting Andy wear it shows his true nature.

jokes.text: Well, it's an original. I have them imported from Italy. It's a Roselli. One of a kind.
jokes.setup: Larry showing confidence about his jacket
jokes.punchline: Hefner revealing it's actually a unique designer piece
jokes.explanation: Sets up the jacket switch plot perfectly. Creates stakes and reveals Larry's mistake, leading to complications.

jokes.text: You ruined my wish. It could have been a coincidence. They ran away from you, Larry.
jokes.setup: Wilson's disappointment at the mansion
jokes.punchline: Blaming Larry specifically for scaring away the women
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Larry's social awkwardness. Wilson's direct blame captures how Larry sabotages situations.

jokes.text: Well, it's not a Roselli but it's a great knock-off.
jokes.setup: Hefner examining Larry's father's jacket
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's a fake version of his designer jacket
jokes.explanation: Key plot revelation. The polite way of saying 'fake' while setting up Larry's embarrassment and the jacket confusion.

jokes.text: I don't think you want to go bowling with me. Let's pretend to be ventriloquists, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry trying to chat up Playboy models
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate conversation topics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry at his most socially clueless. The ventriloquist suggestion is so random and inappropriate it's brilliant.

jokes.text: There's no nude women. They covered all up when they saw me.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking about the Playboy mansion
jokes.punchline: Larry's admission that his presence caused covering up
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware humor. Larry understanding his effect on people and the reversal of the Playboy fantasy is well-crafted.

jokes.text: I have arranged for you to have lunch and play a round of golf with Gary Player.
jokes.setup: The mysterious birthday gift is finally revealed
jokes.punchline: It's something incredibly thoughtful and specific to Larry
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the setup from earlier. Shows Cheryl's thoughtfulness and creates stakes for Larry's behavior.

jokes.text: So I figured, I might as well go to the mansion, so one apology would cover both of them.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his logic
jokes.punchline: His efficiency approach to apologies
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Jeff's theory. Larry's practical application of bad relationship advice is perfectly him.

jokes.text: It's like the dog who pees on the rug. He knows he's getting sent to the doghouse, so on the way, he grabs some food off the kitchen table.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his transgression logic
jokes.punchline: His elaborate dog metaphor for relationship strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of the double transgression theory. The specific dog analogy is creative and memorable.

jokes.text: My jacket is a Roselli jacket. Take a look at this thing. It's a shmata, man. It's not my... this is a knock-off of my jacket.
jokes.setup: Nat examining the returned jacket
jokes.punchline: Discovering it's a cheap imitation of his expensive one
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation that recontextualizes everything. The Yiddish 'shmata' (rag) adds authentic character voice.

jokes.text: Hugh Hefner has your jacket? Hugh Hefner took my jacket?
jokes.setup: Learning where his real jacket is
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of Hugh Hefner accidentally stealing from an old man
jokes.explanation: Great escalation. The celebrity theft angle adds absurdist comedy to the jacket plot.

jokes.text: Who's waiting for you upstairs?
jokes.setup: Hefner dismissing Larry while trying to return the jacket
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate curiosity about Hefner's private activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry boundary violation. Can't help but pry into someone's private affairs even while being dismissed.

jokes.text: If he sits here like a lox for a couple of weeks the organs aren't going to be worth it.
jokes.setup: Larry discussing organ donation with doctor
jokes.punchline: Using food metaphor ('lox') for a coma patient's deterioration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. 'Like a lox' (smoked fish) for someone lying still is both horrible and hilarious. Peak Larry insensitivity.

jokes.text: My wish is for Hugh Hefner's smoking jacket. The red one with the black trim. The Roselli.
jokes.setup: Wilson changing his wish
jokes.punchline: Now wanting the expensive jacket instead of seeing a naked woman
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation. Wilson's learned to want material goods over experiences, showing character growth through greed.

jokes.text: Wilson discovering Larry's house and Larry's panicked reaction
jokes.setup: Wilson sees Larry's mansion while looking for naked women
jokes.punchline: The visual of Wilson's amazement and Larry's horror at being discovered
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Larry being caught in his wealth lie. Wilson's innocence contrasts with Larry's deception.

jokes.text: That's exactly like the jacket they award you when you win the masters. Now there's a 1961 masters jacket.
jokes.setup: Gary Player examining Larry's knockoff jacket
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to his actual Masters tournament jacket
jokes.explanation: Nice irony of the knockoff being compared to actual prestigious jackets. Sets up potential final mix-up.

tmdb_overview: His father's smoking jacket and ""bathroom contamination"" cause grief for Larry.
